title: Microgramma reptans
taxonomy: Microgramma reptans is classified as a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Microgramma, which is a group of epiphytic ferns. Microgramma reptans is further classified as a member of the order Polypodiales and the class Polypodiopsida. This classification is based on its morphological and anatomical characteristics. Microgramma reptans is native to tropical regions of Central and South America.

intro: Microgramma reptans, also known as Polypodium reptans, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, commonly referred to as the "Resurrection Plant" due to its unique ability to revive itself after periods of desiccation. The plant is prized for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it beneficial for treating various health conditions such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Microgramma reptans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Microgramma reptans include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its health benefits and promote overall well-being.
